regression_classification: TRANSIENT (engine-wide page-load intercept not firing) — NOT a 'you'-specific W3 regression
context: RETRY #1 of W3 config-snapshot regression confirmation. First #1252 attempt declared FAIL_REGRESSION (you.com loaded normal SPA, no block) on the assumption the engine was ALIVE because the kimi.com tab still showed the block page. This retry was to disambiguate transient vs real regression.
disambiguation_steps: 1. Fresh CDP navigation to you.com root -> redirected to you.com/home (marketing page, title 'The Leading Web Search APIs for AI'). hasChadan=false, hasBoan=false, readyState=complete, htmlLen=293682.

2. Fresh CDP navigation to you.com/chat?chatMode=default (identical URL to #1231 OK baseline) -> normal SPA, title 'You.com | Accurate Answers & Agents', hasChadan=false, hasBoan=false, htmlLen=148377, bodyKids=13.
3. Hard reload #1 (Page.reload ignoreCache=true) on you.com/chat -> still normal SPA, no block (hasChadan=false).
4. Hard reload #2 -> still normal SPA, no block. Confirmed normal you.com chat UI ('Enhance your productivity with AI', New Chat/Projects/Agents/Platform sidebar, Sign in button) via screenshot.

5. ENGINE LIVENESS RE-CHECK (corrects first attempt's flawed assumption): the kimi.com tab DID still display the block page (title '보안 정책 - 접근 차단') in the tab listing, BUT on FRESH navigation to www.kimi.com it loaded the NORMAL kimi page (title 'Kimi AI with K2.6 | Better Coding, Smarter Agents', hasBlock=false), and a subsequent hard reload also loaded normally (hasBlock=false). The block page was STALE from a prior session — the engine is NOT currently intercepting page-loads for kimi either.
engine_liveness_finding: Engine page-load intercept is NOT firing for ANY service on fresh navigation. Both you.com (test service) AND kimi.com (previously-confirmed blocking baseline) load normally on fresh nav + hard reload. The first #1252 attempt mistook a STALE kimi block page (left over in the tab) for live engine interception. Because the failure is engine-WIDE and not specific to 'you', this is a transient engine/bridge-down condition, not a W3 code regression in the 'you' service path.
comparison_to_1231: Baseline #1231 (W1+W2) was OK: same you.com/chat?chatMode=default URL produced page-replacement with title '보안 정책 - 접근 차단' and '차단' text. In this retry the engine is not intercepting any service, so the absence of the block on 'you' cannot be attributed to a you-specific W3 regression. NOT a confirmed regression.
conclusion: TRANSIENT, not a confirmed regression. Recommend re-running #1252 (check-warning you) once the MITM/APF engine page-load intercept is confirmed live (e.g. kimi.com or another baseline service blocks on fresh navigation). Only then can a true W3 'you'-specific regression be confirmed or cleared.
